mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months signals easier financial conditions, lower discount rates, and a higher present value of equity cash flows, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods after a 3-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent local range indicates labor-market deterioration and slower future household income growth, which pressures equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day returns at least 55% of the time, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST captures stronger current consumer spending and housing demand, which lifts near-term revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise versus their prior 2-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-upturn condition does not outperform either series alone in walk-forward tests, or if positive 5-day returns fail to occur in a majority of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO relative to a stable UNRATE indicates expanding output without labor-market stress, implying stronger real activity and improving earnings breadth that should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction between INDPRO and UNRATE adds no predictive value beyond either series separately, or if 60-day forward returns are not higher when INDPRO rises while UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains below the recent inflation pace, real rates are likely falling and policy is behind the curve, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy spread does not precede positive 20-day returns more often than not, or if the signal turns negative during periods when CPIAUCSL acceleration is strong but equities still rally.

mechanism_narrative: A broad slowdown in GDP and retail sales signals weakening final demand, lower expected corporate revenue growth, and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both fall versus their prior 2-period averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not predict below-average 60-day returns, or if the signal is dominated by one variable and disappears when the other is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS print relative to rising UNRATE captures a labor-market turn from job creation to job destruction, which should depress equity risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-loss and unemployment-rise condition does not lead to negative 5-day returns more often than the base rate, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broader market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO after a prior dip in HOUST signals an industrial rebound with easing inventory overhang and better cyclical growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% over 2 months after HOUST has stabilized or stopped falling
failure_condition: Reject if industrial rebounds after housing stabilization do not predict higher 60-day returns than other periods, or if the effect is absent when INDPRO is isolated from HOUST.
